title: Standardize array query parameter encoding in XRPC
description:
The reference implementation's makeParams() function supports []string values and encodes them as repeated query parameters. Our implementation handles arrays but the encoding is inconsistent across different request paths.

**Reference:** reference/indigo/xrpc/xrpc.go (makeParams function, lines 117-133)

**Fix:** Standardize array query parameter encoding to use repeated params format.

title: Add XRPCError class for client-side error handling
description:
The reference implementation has XRPCError struct for parsing error responses. Our implementation only has server-side error handling, but lacks a client-side error class.

**Reference:** reference/indigo/xrpc/xrpc.go (XRPCError struct, lines 50-57)

**Impact:** Inconsistent error handling for XRPC consumers.

**Fix:** Add XRPCError class that parses error and message fields from JSON response body.

title: Add missing ATProtoValidator class to integration-tests
description:
Main branch added ATProtoValidator.h/m for DID, Handle, CID, TID, NSID validation.

Integration-tests worktree needs this file for spec-compliant identifier validation.

Copy from main branch commit d2a2273.

title: Transfer-Encoding: chunked support
description:
Implement HTTP/1.1 chunked transfer encoding per RFC 9112 Section 7.1.

Currently HttpServer only supports Content-Length bodies. Need to handle Transfer-Encoding: chunked for streaming uploads without known content length.

Acceptance criteria:
- Parse chunked transfer encoding in HTTP parser
- Reassemble chunked body into complete NSData before processing
- Support streaming mode for large uploads (write to temp file)
- Return 411 Length Required when Content-Length expected but missing
- Test with curl sending chunked data: curl -H "Transfer-Encoding: chunked" -d "chunk1\r\nchunk2" http://localhost/

This enables streaming uploads where content length is unknown upfront.

title: Fix XrpcMethodRegistry.m to use new PDSController API
description: XrpcMethodRegistry.m calls methods that don't exist on the new PDSController interface. Need to update method calls to use new API or backward-compatible methods.

title: Buffer pooling for request/response objects
description:
Implement slab allocator for HttpRequest/HttpResponse objects and request buffers to reduce allocations under load. Currently each request creates new objects; pooling allows reuse.

Acceptance criteria:
- HttpBufferPool class with fixed-size slabs
- Reuse HttpRequest/HttpResponse objects from pool
- Thread-safe acquire/release API
- Configurable pool sizes (e.g., 64, 256, 1024 byte buffers)
- Integration with HttpConnectionState

This reduces memory churn and improves performance under high request rates.

docs/guides/DEVELOPMENT_WORKFLOWS.md - 8 diagrams for dev processes
docs/guides/XRPC_PROTOCOL_REFERENCE.md - Protocol quick reference

These files enhance documentation and should be preserved when merging.

These headers should either: (1) work on both platforms, or (2) use #ifndef __APPLE__ guards properly.

2. Replace NSJSONSerialization with [ATProtoCBORSerialization encodeDataWithJSONObject:error:]
3. Test that CID generation produces spec-compliant output

See main branch commit 4b3f6af for reference implementation.

title: PLC: Add test vectors for operation verification
description:
## Summary
The PLC implementation lacks test vectors to verify correctness against the reference implementation. The reference has test vectors in reference/indigo/plc/client_test.go.

## Background
- Reference implementation: reference/indigo/plc/client_test.go
- Our implementation: No test vectors
- Impact: Cannot verify interoperability with reference implementation

## Requirements
- Add test vectors for CreateOp CBOR encoding
- Add test vectors for DID calculation
- Add test vectors for signature verification
- Compare output with reference implementation

## Test Vectors to Add:
1. Create operation CBOR encoding (base64 encoded)
2. DID calculation (SHA256 + Base32 of CBOR)
3. Signature verification (secp256k1)

## Files to modify:
- ATProtoPDS/Tests/PLC/PLCOperationTests.m

## References
- reference/indigo/plc/client_test.go (testVector struct)
- reference/indigo/atproto/identity/testdata/did_plc_doc.json

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test vectors match reference implementation
- [ ] CBOR encoding verified against test vector
- [ ] DID calculation verified against test vector
- [ ] Signature verification verified against test vector
- [ ] All tests pass

title: Add rate limit response headers to XRPC responses
description:
The reference implementation parses and returns rate limit headers (ratelimit-limit, ratelimit-remaining, ratelimit-reset, ratelimit-policy) so clients can implement backoff strategies. Our implementation has rate limiting but doesn't return these headers.

**Reference:** reference/indigo/xrpc/xrpc.go (errorFromHTTPResponse function)

**Impact:** Clients cannot implement proper backoff strategies when rate limited.

**Fix:** Add X-RateLimit-* headers to response when rate limit is exceeded in XrpcHandler.m

title: Restore CBOR map sorting test in IdentifierTests
description:
Main branch added testCBORMapSorting test that verifies DAG-CBOR map key sorting.

Integration-tests branch has the older version of IdentifierTests.m without this test.

The test verifies that { 'b': 2, 'a': 1 } encodes the same as { 'a': 1, 'b': 2 } - required for deterministic CID generation.

See main branch commit d2a2273.

title: Replace CommonCrypto with OpenSSL

Integration-tests worktree needs this file to properly implement CBOR-based CID calculation.

Copy from main branch or create based on main branch implementation.

See main branch commit 4b3f6af.

title: PLC: Implement persistent storage backend
description:
## Summary
The PLC implementation only has a mock in-memory store (PLCMockStore). We need a persistent storage backend for production use.

## Background
The reference implementation (reference/indigo/plc/fakedid.go) uses GORM with SQLite for persistence. Our implementation has a PLCStore protocol but only a mock implementation.

## Requirements
- Implement PLCPersistentStore using SQLite (similar to PDSActorStore)
- Support the PLCStore protocol:
  - getHistoryForDID(did) -> NSArray<PLCOperation *>
  - appendOperation(op) -> BOOL
- Use WAL mode for performance
- Prepared statements for query optimization
- Proper indexing on DID column

## Files to create/modify:
- ATProtoPDS/Sources/PLC/PLCPersistentStore.h
- ATProtoPDS/Sources/PLC/PLCPersistentStore.m
- Update main.m to use PLCPersistentStore when database path is provided

## References
- reference/indigo/plc/fakedid.go for storage pattern
- PDSActorStore for SQLite patterns in this codebase

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] PLCPersistentStore implements PLCStore protocol
- [ ] Uses SQLite with WAL mode
- [ ] Proper indexing for performance
- [ ] main.m accepts --database parameter and uses persistent store
- [ ] All existing PLC tests pass

title: Fix OAuth2Tests
description: Update OAuth2Tests to use OAuth2Server class and fix property access.

title: PLC: Implement caching layer for DID resolution
description:
## Summary
The PLC implementation lacks caching for DID document resolution, which can impact performance under load. The reference implementation has both in-memory ARC cache and memcached support.

## Background
- Reference implementation: reference/indigo/plc/caching.go and memcached.go
- Our implementation: No caching layer
- Impact: Every DID resolution requires full history replay and signature verification

## Requirements
- Implement PLCCacheDirectory similar to reference/indigo/atproto/identity/cache_directory.go
- Cache DID document resolution results
- Implement cache invalidation (FlushCacheFor)
- Support configurable cache size and TTL
- Integrate with PLCStore protocol

## Implementation Options:
1. In-memory cache (simpler, for single-instance deployment)
2. Redis-backed cache (for distributed deployment)

## Files to create:
- ATProtoPDS/Sources/PLC/PLCCacheDirectory.h
- ATProtoPDS/Sources/PLC/PLCCacheDirectory.m

## References
- reference/indigo/plc/caching.go
- reference/indigo/atproto/identity/cache_directory.go

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] PLCCacheDirectory wraps PLCStore
- [ ] Caches DID document resolution
- [ ] Configurable TTL and cache size
- [ ] Cache invalidation on updates
- [ ] Performance improvement measured

title: Rewrite Guides & Research documentation files (developer guides, research docs, troubleshooting)
Rewrite Security & Testing documentation files (security reports, test plans, vulnerability reports)
Fix streaming event CBOR format for subscribeRepos
description:
The reference implementation uses EventHeader with Op and MsgType for streaming events, with proper message types like #commit, #identity, #account, #info. Our implementation uses a plain JSON-like dictionary format.

**Reference:** reference/indigo/events/events.go (EventHeader and XRPCStreamEvent)

**Impact:** Our streaming events are not compliant with ATProto specification.

**Fix:** Implement proper EventHeader envelope with Op and MsgType fields, use proper CBOR encoding for streaming events.

title: PLC: Implement handle update operation
description:
## Summary
Neither the reference nor our implementation fully supports handle updates. The reference has a stub that returns an error.

## Background
- Reference: UpdateUserHandle returns "not yet implemented" error
- Our implementation: No handle update operation
- Impact: Users cannot change their handle after initial creation

## Requirements
- Support "update_handle" operation type
- Verify signature with rotation keys
- Update alsoKnownAs in DID document
- Validate new handle format (syntax.Handle validation)

## Operation Type:
```json
{
  "type": "update_handle",
  "handle": "newhandle.bsky.social",
  "prev": "<previous operation hash>"
}
```

## Files to modify:
- ATProtoPDS/Sources/PLC/PLCOperation.m (PLCStateReplayer)
- ATProtoPDS/Sources/PLC/PLCAuditor.m (verification)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] update_handle operation accepted
- [ ] Signature verified with rotation keys
- [ ] Handle format validated
- [ ] alsoKnownAs updated in DID document
- [ ] Integration tests pass

title: PLC: Add observability and metrics
description:
## Summary
The PLC implementation lacks observability and metrics. The reference implementation has Prometheus metrics for cache hits/misses.

## Background
- Reference implementation: reference/indigo/plc/metrics.go
- Our implementation: No metrics
- Impact: No visibility into PLC operation performance and usage

## Requirements
- Implement PLCMetrics similar to reference/indigo/plc/metrics.go
- Track metrics:
  - Cache hits and misses
  - DID resolution latency
  - Operation submission rate
  - Verification failures
- Expose metrics endpoint (HTTP /_metrics)
- Integrate with existing observability infrastructure

## Files to create:
- ATProtoPDS/Sources/PLC/PLCMetrics.h
- ATProtoPDS/Sources/PLC/PLCMetrics.m

## Files to modify:
- ATProtoPDS/Sources/PLC/PLCServer.m (add metrics collection)

## References
- reference/indigo/plc/metrics.go
- Existing metrics patterns in the codebase

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Metrics for cache hits/misses
- [ ] Metrics for operation verification
- [ ] Metrics for resolution latency
- [ ] /_metrics endpoint exposed
- [ ] Metrics integrated with existing infrastructure
